name: Naan (Indian Flat Bread)
recipeInstructions:
If desired, you can use cornmeal, sesame seeds, or poppy seeds on the bread.
Combine 1 cup flour, yeast, and salt in a mixing bowl. Stir in water, buttermilk, egg, oil, and honey. Beat until smooth with an electric mixer. Stir in enough remaining flour to form a soft, sticky dough. Turn onto a floured surface, continue to work in flour until the dough is stiff enough to knead. Knead until smooth and elastic, but still soft (3 to 5 minutes). Place in an oiled bowl; turn once to coat the top of the dough. Cover; let rise until doubled in bulk (about 45 minutes). Punch dough down. Shape into 16 equal balls. Let rest 5 minutes. Roll out each ball to a 1/4-inch thick round. If desired, brush with melted butter and sprinkle with garnishes. Set on baking sheets. Bake at 450°F for 5 to 8 minutes. NOTE: There is no second rising in this recipe so that you get flat loaves.
